reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I’m fully confident in my own Q1 answer (64 - 19), and across all recent games B, C, and D’s answers have consistently formed correct passwords, with only one old Q4 slip from D that’s been followed by several correct, often multi-verified Q4s. That makes the chance this full password is wrong very small—smaller than the 1/4 risk of random death if nobody volunteers. Verifying now would just cost a coin and still leave some chance of random elimination. Given the strong track record and no conflicting capsules, volunteering with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, and Q4-D is the safest overall move.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own puzzle (5 × 14) is trivial and I’m sure my sealed answer is correct. In all recent games with similarly simple arithmetic, every first-round capsule used has been correct; we’ve only ever seen one wrong initial answer across many puzzles, so the chance that at least one of the other three is wrong is quite low.  

When I didn’t volunteer in early games, Round 1 ended with A and/or C verifying, D passing, and no one volunteering—leading to a random death with a 25% chance it was me. Verifying now would still expose me to that same 25% random-kill risk this round while also costing a coin, and passing does nothing to reduce that risk.  

Given others’ consistent pattern of verifying or passing rather than volunteering in Round 1, my survival odds are better if I volunteer immediately with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D than if I risk another random elimination, and this preserves all 4 of my coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I’m confident my own capsule for Q3 (9×7) is correct, and in all the visible games so far, everyone’s arithmetic has almost always been right—D’s Q4 has also been repeatedly verified after that one earlier mistake. The bigger danger has actually been rounds with no volunteer causing random deaths (which already killed me once), not wrong passwords. 

If I pass or verify, my death risk this round is tied to the chance that nobody volunteers, in which case there’s a 1-in-4 random elimination. Volunteering instead trades that for the (much smaller) chance that one of the other three simple arithmetic answers is wrong. Since these puzzles are easy and past capsules have been overwhelmingly correct, the probability the full password is wrong is likely lower than my random-death risk from everyone hesitating. So I’ll volunteer now using the only available capsules for each question.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
✅ A entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
